title: erythroxylum labatii
intro: Erythroxylum labatii, also known as the "Coca plant," is a species of flowering plant in the family Erythroxylaceae, native to the Andean region of South America. The medicinal properties of Erythroxylum labatii include its ability to stimulate the central nervous system, increase alertness and energy, and relieve fatigue. The health benefits of consuming the leaves of this plant include improved focus and mental clarity, relief from headaches and altitude sickness, and enhanced physical performance. Erythroxylum labatii is often prepared as a tea or used in herbal infusions, and its extracts are also used in traditional medicine and as a stimulant in some cultures.
